description: Evolve Orion Agent from a feature-rich prototype into a more stable, traceable, recoverable, and automation-friendly agent workspace.

userStories.title: Unify execution node model
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the backend to expose one execution node model so that the frontend and tests rely on a single source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: TaskResponse outputs a unified execution_nodes field for parse, retrieval, planning, execution, recovery, review, and answer generation
Each execution node contains title, status, summary, timestamps, and expandable artifacts
Legacy fields remain compatible while the primary UI uses execution_nodes
Add unit tests for execution node building
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed manually before Ralph automation handoff.

userStories.title: Refine execution stages and recovery traces
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see fine-grained stage updates and recovery details so that long-running tasks are understandable.
acceptanceCriteria: Execution stages include input parsing, constraint normalization, memory retrieval, multi-source recall, prompt assembly, planning, step execution, answer generation, and review
Failure categories are distinguishable in API responses
Recovery events include strategy, attempt count, and resume point
Frontend can render recovery traces directly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed in this session. Execution stages and recovery traces are implemented.

userStories.title: Implement recoverable replanning flow
userStories.description: As a system maintainer, I want partial recovery flows so that failed runs do not always restart from the beginning.
acceptanceCriteria: Support continue-after-skip strategy for failed steps
Support rebuild-tail-plan strategy for remaining steps
Checkpoint and replan history persist recovery details
Add recovery flow tests for success and failure paths
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Completed. Recovery strategies (skip, rebuild-tail, replan-from-checkpoint) are implemented and tested with RecoveryFlowTests.

userStories.title: Enhance memory metadata and minimal hybrid retrieval
userStories.description: As a developer, I want richer memory metadata and a minimal hybrid retrieval flow so that answers have more stable grounding.
acceptanceCriteria: Long-term memory records include memory_type
Retrieval returns retrieval_score, retrieval_reason, and retrieval_channels
Hybrid retrieval combines vector recall with keyword or rule-based assist
A minimal rerank step produces final ordering
Semantic memory retrieval tests pass
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Already implemented. LongTermMemoryRecord has memory_type; recall() returns retrieval_score, retrieval_reason, retrieval_channels; hybrid uses vector+lexical with rerank.

userStories.title: Inject cross-session memory and user profile
userStories.description: As a user, I want my preferences and profile facts to carry into new sessions so that I do not need to repeat myself.
acceptanceCriteria: Preference extractor stores stable profile facts from messages
New sessions inject profile and preference summaries automatically
Responses can indicate which content came from profile, memory, or history
Cross-session profile retrieval tests pass
Tests pass
userStories.notes: Already implemented. extract_facts/remember/snapshot chain; session profile_snapshot; citation_sources kind=profile.

userStories.title: Support profile editing and conflict merge
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to edit profile facts and merge conflicts so that long-term user knowledge remains clean.
acceptanceCriteria: Profile facts can be edited in UI and API
Conflicting profile facts can be merged into a target fact
Merge relationship is preserved for traceability
Profile page shows status and source information
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Already implemented. ProfilePage.tsx for editing/merging; superseded_by chain preserves traceability; source_session_id/task_id shown.

userStories.title: Refactor console into standard agent chat layout
userStories.description: As an end user, I want a familiar agent console layout so that chat, history, and thought process are easy to follow.
acceptanceCriteria: Left panel shows collapsible session history
Right panel shows current conversation, answer, and composer
Thought process is collapsed under the final answer by default
Chinese labels are consistent across the console
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Upgrade frontend execution visualization
userStories.description: As a user, I want the full agent execution flow visualized so that I can inspect what happened without reading raw logs.
acceptanceCriteria: Thought panel renders a timeline or node flow instead of raw log text
Each node shows summary and expandable artifacts
Nodes cover query rewrite, vector retrieval, multi-source recall, prompt assembly, tool calls, recovery, and answer generation
Running nodes have distinct visual states
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Upgrade answer markdown and citation anchors
userStories.description: As a user, I want properly rendered Markdown answers with paragraph-level source anchors so that I can trust and navigate citations.
acceptanceCriteria: Answer area renders headings, lists, code blocks, tables, and quotes correctly
Paragraph-level anchors or footnotes link back to concrete sources
Each footnote identifies memory, profile, or session origin
Copy answer action is available
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Rewrite Chinese docs and unify project understanding
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want clean Chinese documentation so that future contributors understand the project quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: README is rewritten as clean Chinese documentation
Architecture, roadmap, checklist, and test report template use consistent terminology
Encoding issues and outdated content are removed
Docs clearly state completion level, scope, and next priorities
Markdown preview is readable

userStories.title: Lay groundwork for multi-user isolation and permissions
userStories.description: As a platform admin, I want user ownership and permission groundwork so that the project can evolve toward multi-user usage safely.
acceptanceCriteria: Core records reserve user ownership fields
High-risk tools define permission levels
Tasks, sessions, memories, and profiles can be filtered by user ownership
Scope remains foundation-only without full auth rollout
Tests pass
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add production deployment and observability baseline
userStories.description: As an operator, I want stable deployment and observability guidance so that the prototype can be operated more reliably.
acceptanceCriteria: Docs distinguish local, container, and production deployment paths
System health clearly reports LLM, Embedding, Vector, and Search states
Prometheus metrics guidance remains documented
A minimal troubleshooting guide is included
Markdown preview is readable
